basics.name: Tuna Gürbüz
basics.label: Graduate Electrical & Computer Engineer
basics.summary: My research centers on 3D scene understanding and multimodal perception, with a focus on leveraging vision foundation models for learned representations enabling geometrically precise and semantically rich reasoning about the world in multi-view environments.

highlights: Master Thesis: Multiview Panoptic Operating Room Segmentation (Grade: 1.0)
Bachelor Thesis: Shared Control Based on Both Tracking Performance and Human Torque for Robotic Rehabilitation (MATLAB/Simulink, Grade: 1.7)

work.name: Informatics 16, Technical University of Munich
work.position: Master Thesis Researcher — Multiview Panoptic Operating Room Segmentation
highlights: Investigated 3D feature lifting using NeRFs and voxel networks for panoptic segmentation.
Explored vision foundation models (SAM, DINO, CLIP) for multimodal understanding.
Adapted VGGT's aggregator for spatial understanding, achieving +5% Panoptic Quality (PQ) improvement with the video model and +7% PQ improvement with the multi-view model on MM-OR and 4D-OR datasets.

work.name: Huawei Research Center
work.position: Intern / Working Student — Deep Learning for Communications
highlights: Built an autoencoder FFN (PyTorch) with a model-based decoder for signal covariance estimation, outperforming baseline by ~+7 dB SNR.
Built an automated synthetic data generation, model training and evaluation pipeline.
Trained a transformer model with unique learnable position embeddings for beamspace phase error estimation achieving similar performance as the PNPC algorithm in significantly lower time.

work.name: Chair of Cognitive Systems, Technical University of Munich
work.position: Research Intern — Semantic Mapping with Quest 3 AR/VR
highlights: Developed a Unity (C#) app for 6-DoF tracking and scene mesh extraction on Quest 3.
Integrated ROS# for real-time wireless transfer to master computer for ray tracing from headset pose in the scene mesh with <10 ms latency.
Deployed Mask2Former (Detectron2) on the master computer for real-time panoptic segmentation, achieving mesh labeling at 15 Hz throughput using NVIDIA RTX3090.

work.name: Chair of Information-oriented Control, Technical University of Munich
work.position: Bachelor Thesis Researcher — Shared Control for Robotic Rehabilitation
highlights: Simulated a 2-DoF robot arm's dynamics in MATLAB and designed PD + gravity compensation-based impedance controllers for position tracking.
Implemented an assist-as-needed strategy adapting controller gains online based on human performance.

work.name: AED Vantage
highlights: Developed C++ modules for the IPBasis ECU of the new BMW vehicle generation.

work.name: TUfast Student Racing Team
highlights: Designed schematics and layout for centralized A/D boards and microcontroller programming in C++.
Won 3rd place at Formula Student Germany (FSG) 2022.

publications.name: PanORama: Multiview Consistent Panoptic Segmentation in Operating Rooms
publications.summary: Multiview consistent panoptic segmentation framework for operating room scene understanding.

publications.name: TransPNPC: Transformer-based Deep Unfolding for Phase Noise Parameter Compensation in Beamspace DOA Estimation
publications.summary: Transformer-based deep unfolding approach for phase noise compensation in beamspace DOA estimation.
